conflict: Museum leadership wants polished legacy copy while Salvador Rivas uses his declining health to demand unsupervised interviews on his terms.
consequence: Marisol accepts Salvador's private-access bargain, making her project dependent on his account of her family history.
must_land_beats: Marisol marks 30 months sober before entering the museum.
Chief curator Nora frames the commission as reputation management for donors.
Salvador recognizes Gloria's surname and asks for closed-door sessions.
Marisol chooses access over caution and commits to interview him alone.
objective: Secure the Brooklyn Museum residency launch and gather viable material for Marisol's martyr manuscript.
